description:
Advances: specled.coverage_capture

## Two related defects, one default

**1. The default snapshot produces an artifact with no per-test line signal — on the intended path, not just under misuse.** Coverage.init/2 defaults snapshot_fn to apply(:cover, :analyse, [:_]). :cover.analyse/1 defaults to *function-level* analysis, so entries are {{m,f,a}, {cov, not_cov}}; the decoder fallback clause (group_by_file in formatter.ex) maps those to {file, 0}, discarding the counts. mix spec.cover.test installs the formatter with no opts, so the intended serialized path inherits this default too. Yet coverage_capture.spec.md summarizes the subject as "captures per-test **line** coverage" — the spec and the default implementation disagree.

Empirical (decoded from a real .spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata produced by the voyd_config suite on 2026-07-23): 381,142 records; all 1,279 tests carry the identical 298-file list; every record has lines_hit == [0]. Zero per-test discrimination.

**2. The default cost is O(tests x cover-compiled modules) in both time and memory.** One full-project analyse per test_finished, with each complete snapshot retained in ETS until suite_finished. Downstream measurement (voyd_config, 538 modules): ~0.45-0.70 s per test on ubuntu-latest → ~17 min added silently to every CI run at 1,480 tests; locally ~6 min and ~6.5 GB peak RSS at 1,273 tests. Because ExUnit delivers test_finished as async casts and stops formatters with timeout :infinity, the backlog drains *after* the CLI summary prints — from the outside it presents as a CI hang (that is how it was found: voyd_config CI runs 29993186732 / 29997031593).

## Aggravating factor: nothing detects off-spec adoption

voyd_config (commit 0d17034) wired the formatter directly into test_helper.exs under mix coveralls.json with async: true — exactly what ADR specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage says is unsupported. The mix task warns about async files, but the formatter itself runs silently in that configuration: no warning, no refusal, just wrong data at maximum cost. Adopters get no signal that they are off the supported path.

## Ask

Decide what per-test coverage capture should actually promise, and make the default either deliver it affordably or fail loudly. Deliberately NOT prescribing a fix — directions worth exploring, none validated:

- Reconcile spec vs. implementation: either the default snapshot yields real line-level data that the decoder preserves, or the spec summary stops claiming line coverage. A loud failure (or at least a warning) when snapshots degrade to file-level zeros would have surfaced this immediately.
- Whether runtime coverage is needed for triangulation at all — tagged_tests + test_tags already give explicit, async-safe, zero-cost test-to-subject mapping. Runtime capture might be opt-in enrichment rather than a default-on formatter.
- OTP 27+ native coverage (:code.get_coverage/2, :code.reset_coverage/1) for cheap per-boundary deltas if genuine per-test line data is the requirement — potentially cheap enough to revisit the serialized-only ADR.
- Scoped snapshots via the existing knobs (snapshot_fn, snapshot_target, modules_fn) plus a documented cost model, so adopters cannot inherit the pathological default unknowingly.
- Guardrails in the formatter itself: detect async execution / invocation outside spec.cover.test and warn or refuse, rather than relying on the stderr scan in the mix task.
- Backlog safety: batching, sampling, or a suite-size guard so formatter drain can never silently add tens of minutes after the ExUnit summary.

## Evidence pointers

- Dep-free synthetic repro (200 modules, 60 tests, elixir:1.18 Docker): formatter+cover → 57.5 s silent gap; no formatter → 0.0008 s; no cover → 0.04 s; 20 tests → 22.0 s (linear in test count).
- Relevant code: lib/specled_ex/coverage.ex (default_snapshot_fn/0, init/2), lib/specled_ex/coverage/formatter.ex (record_test/2, group_by_file/1 fallback, flush/1), lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex (install_formatter/0 — no opts).
- Downstream incident: voyd_config commit 0d17034; CI runs 29993186732 / 29997031593 (17-min hang signature; gap scales with test count across six measured jobs).

comments.text:
Root cause of the downstream misadoption found: docs/adoption.md itself instructs the unsupported wiring — twice (Step 4 "Wire per-test coverage into the test helper", lines 130-135, and Phase 4 "Coverage triangulation", lines 292-299), both showing ExUnit.start(formatters: [ExUnit.CLIFormatter, SpecLedEx.Coverage.Formatter]) in test/test_helper.exs. voyd_config commit 0d17034 followed this guide verbatim.

The instruction is doubly wrong:
1. A formatter in ExUnit.start activates on EVERY test invocation (plain mix test, mix coveralls.json), not just mix spec.cover.test — exactly the async/unserialized configuration the coverage_capture spec and ADR specled.decision.serialized_per_test_coverage declare unsupported.
2. It is redundant even for the supported path: mix spec.cover.test appends the formatter itself via ExUnit.configure before running the test task, and a plain ExUnit.start() picks up that persisted config. Verified: reverting the downstream test_helper.exs to ExUnit.start() restores fast CI while keeping mix spec.cover.test fully functional.

Whatever fix direction this issue lands on, docs/adoption.md needs the test_helper wiring instruction removed/corrected — it is the vector by which adopters inherit the pathological default.

title: Coverage.Formatter default snapshot: O(tests x modules) cost, artifact carries no line data despite spec claim
